description:
You are working as a storage administrator and need to reclaim space under 
    /home/user/logs while ensuring applications that depend on the original 
    paths keep functioning.

    INITIAL STATE VISIBLE TO YOU
    --------------------------------
    /home/user/logs/
        ├── app1/
        │   ├── log1.txt   (a small text file)
        │   └── log2.txt   (a small text file)
        └── app2/
            └── logA.txt   (a small text file)

    GOAL
    ----
    1. Create an archive area under /home/user called archive.
    2. Relocate the whole app2 directory into that archive area **without** 
       changing its name, but place it under a new directory called app2_logs.  
       (The resulting path on disk must be /home/user/archive/app2_logs/…).
    3. Replace the original /home/user/logs/app2 directory with a **symbolic 
       link** (not a hard-link) named “app2” that points to the *new* location.
       • The link itself must live in /home/user/logs/  
       • The link target must be an **absolute path**.
    4. Inside the still-existing /home/user/logs/app1 directory, create a 
       **relative** symbolic link called old_latest that points to log2.txt.
       (Use a *relative* target string such that the link remains valid if 
       /home/user/logs/app1 is moved as a whole.)
    5. Produce an audit file at /home/user/symlink_audit.log describing every 
       symbolic link you created in *exactly* the format below:

       <full_link_path> -> <absolute_resolved_target_path>

       • One link per line.  
       • <full_link_path> must be the absolute path of the link itself  
       • <absolute_resolved_target_path> must be the absolute path the link 
         ultimately resolves to after the filesystem follows the link.  
       • No extra whitespace before or after the arrow (->).  
       • End the file with a single trailing newline.

       Example of the required formatting **ONLY FOR ILLUSTRATION**
       /path/to/link -> /real/target/path\n

    SUCCESS CRITERIA (what the automated tests will verify)
    -------------------------------------------------------
    A. Directory /home/user/archive exists.  
    B. Directory /home/user/archive/app2_logs contains logA.txt.  
    C. The path /home/user/logs/app2 is a *symbolic link* whose target string 
       is an absolute path equal to /home/user/archive/app2_logs.  
    D. The path /home/user/logs/app1/old_latest is a *symbolic link* whose 
       resolved target is /home/user/logs/app1/log2.txt and whose stored 
       target string is relative (does not start with “/”).  
    E. /home/user/symlink_audit.log exists and contains exactly two lines:
       1) /home/user/logs/app2            -> /home/user/archive/app2_logs
       2) /home/user/logs/app1/old_latest -> /home/user/logs/app1/log2.txt
       (with a single trailing newline, no blank lines, and lines in any order).

    Complexity target: The whole job should be doable with roughly 5-10 
    standard shell commands (mkdir, mv, ln, etc.).  You may, of course, use 
    additional commands for inspection or debugging, but the core actions 
    should fit within that range.

    IMPORTANT:
    • Do not delete any log files.  
    • Do not compress the files.  
    • Only symbolic links should be used—no hard links.  
    • Absolute vs. relative link requirements are strict.  
    • Follow the exact audit-file format so automated grading succeeds.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data the grader will use -->
    <!-- Initial files with their contents (already present before task starts) -->
    /home/user/logs/app1/log1.txt: "Error ABC\n"
    /home/user/logs/app1/log2.txt: "Warning XYZ\n"
    /home/user/logs/app2/logA.txt: "Debug 123\n"

    <!-- Items that MUST exist after correct completion -->
    dir:/home/user/archive
    dir:/home/user/archive/app2_logs
    file:/home/user/archive/app2_logs/logA.txt: "Debug 123\n"

    symlink:/home/user/logs/app2 -> /home/user/archive/app2_logs (stored target string must be "/home/user/archive/app2_logs")

    symlink:/home/user/logs/app1/old_latest (stored target string must be "log2.txt")  # relative
                                              -> /home/user/logs/app1/log2.txt        # resolved

    file:/home/user/symlink_audit.log:
        (either order, but exactly these two lines with trailing newline)
        "/home/user/logs/app2 -> /home/user/archive/app2_logs\n"
        "/home/user/logs/app1/old_latest -> /home/user/logs/app1/log2.txt\n"
